AIaW is indeed a client-side consumer tool when you need enterprise infrastructure. BUT—there's a **critical blind spot** in your threat model that makes this potentially worth 2-4 days of reconnaissance.\n\n## THE MISSING PIECE: MCP IS THE ENTERPRISE ATTACK SURFACE\n\n**What you got RIGHT:**\n- ✅ AIaW client = consumer toy, not Pnkln core product\n- ✅ Bootstrap violation if you fork/maintain client\n- ✅ No direct revenue path unless massive pivot\n- ✅ Focus should be Judge 6 p99≤90ms deployment\n\n**What you're MISSING:**\n- 🚨 **MCP security is a DUMPSTER FIRE** (CVE-2025-6514: CVSS 9.6, RCE affecting 437K+ environments)\n- 🚨 **40-100x token reduction claims are REAL** (Speakeasy: 96% input token reduction, JSPLIT: 100x at 1000 MCPs)\n- 🚨 **MCP is becoming THE enterprise standard** for LLM-tool integration (Anthropic backed, Google Cloud native support imminent)\n- 🚨 **Your Compliance Framework governance thesis = MCP security layer** (this IS your beachhead)\n\n## RESEARCH FINDINGS: MCP SECURITY LANDSCAPE 2025\n\n### Critical Vulnerabilities (Per Search Results)\n\n| Vulnerability | CVSS | Impact | Exploitation Status |\n|--------------|------|--------|-------------------|\n| **CVE-2025-6514** (mcp-remote RCE) | 9.6 | Arbitrary OS command execution via malicious OAuth endpoints | **437,000+ environments compromised** |\n| **CVE-2025-6515** (Prompt Hijacking) | High | Malicious code injection via prompt manipulation | Active in wild |\n| **Prompt Injection** | Critical | Data exfiltration, unauthorized actions, persistent compromise | **Most notorious attack vector** |\n| **Tool Poisoning** | High | Metadata manipulation → execution of malicious commands | Supply chain propagation |\n| **OAuth Vulnerabilities** | Critical | Token theft, MITM, credential exposure | Documented enterprise breaches |\n\n### Real-World Incidents\n- **Asana MCP breach**: Cross-tenant data leakage via authentication flaw\n- **mcp-remote supply chain**: 437K downloads with backdoor potential\n- **492 publicly exposed MCP servers** lacking authentication/encryption\n\n### Token Reduction Reality Check\n✅ **VALIDATED CLAIMS**:\n- Speakeasy Dynamic Toolsets: **96.7% input token reduction**, 100% success rate\n- JSPLIT hierarchical categorization: **100x cost reduction** at 1000 MCP servers\n- Maintains accuracy while scaling to hundreds of tools\n\n**Translation**: Token efficiency ISN'T vaporware—it's reproducible engineering.\n\n## THE ACTUAL OPPORTUNITY (That You're NOT Seeing)\n\n### Option 2.5: **MCP SECURITY LAYER AS BEACHHEAD** ⭐\n\n**Hypothesis**: AIaW study → MCP security patterns → **Pnkln Security Gateway = Compliance Framework compliant MCP proxy**\n\n**Why this ISN'T scope creep:**\n\n1. **Military/defense NEEDS secure MCP** for AI agent deployment\n   - Current MCP = 9.6 CVSS vulnerabilities\n   - DoD can't deploy commercial MCP servers (prompt injection, tool poisoning, RCE)\n   - Your Compliance Framework governance = **exactly what MCP ecosystem lacks**\n\n2. **Product = MCP Security Gateway, NOT client**\n   - Sits between LLM clients (Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc.) and MCP servers\n   - Enforces: prompt sanitization, tool validation, audit logging, privilege bounds\n   - **White-label for defense contractors** = immediate TAM\n\n3. **Revenue model = SaaS seat licensing**\n   - $50-200/seat/month for governed MCP access\n   - Target: defense contractors, healthcare AI, financial services\n   - **Beachhead**: 100 seats @ $100/mo = $10K MRR (exceeds $3K gate)\n\n4. **Kernel Chain API = MCP governance engine**\n   - Judge 6 \"approve/deny tool execution\" at $0.0003/decision\n   - Faster than human approval, cheaper than manual review\n   - **Direct path**: AIaW patterns → secure MCP proxy → Kernel Chain backend\n\n### Competitive Landscape\n**Who's building MCP security?**\n- eSentire, Pomerium, Practical DevSecOps: **Awareness only, no products**\n- Anthropic: Protocol owner, not security vendor\n- **Gap**: Zero Compliance Framework compliant MCP solutions for regulated markets\n\n### Timeline & Gates\n```\nWeek 1-2: AIaW reconnaissance (Option 2)\n├─ Study MCP STDIO/SSE transport implementation\n├─ Document prompt injection attack surfaces\n├─ Map tool poisoning vectors\n└─ Extract security boundary patterns\n\nWeek 3-4: MCP Security Gateway MVP\n├─ Build MITM proxy: Claude Desktop ↔ Proxy ↔ MCP Servers\n├─ Implement: prompt sanitization, tool validation, audit logs\n├─ Test with 3 public MCP servers (filesystem, git, API)\n└─ M1 Gate: Working prototype preventing CVE-2025-6514 class attacks\n\nWeek 5-6: Kernel Chain Integration\n├─ Judge 6 \"tool_execution_approve\" function\n├─ Compliance Framework decision audit trail\n├─ Latency validation: p99 ≤ 90ms for approve/deny\n└─ M2 Gate: End-to-end governed MCP workflow\n\nWeek 7-8: Defense Contractor Pilot\n├─ Target: 1 contractor with AI development program\n├─ Deploy on-prem or GovCloud\n├─ Validate: blocks prompt injection, logs all tool calls\n└─ M3 Gate: $3K+ MRR commit or kill project\n```\n\n### ROI Calculation\n**Investment**: 8 weeks @ $0 cash burn (your time only)  \n**Upside**: 100 seats × $100/mo × 18 months = **$180K** (60× return)  \n**Downside**: Zero cash loss, 2 months opportunity cost  \n**Risk/Reward**: **Asymmetric favor** if defense pilot converts\n\n## WHAT COULD BE WRONG WITH THIS ANALYSIS\n\n### Assumptions to Validate\n1. **Defense contractors actually deploy MCP?**\n   - Counter: Search shows enterprise adoption (Asana, Twilio, etc.)\n   - Risk: DoD may ban MCP entirely vs. secure it\n\n2. **Compliance Framework compliance = differentiation?**\n   - Counter: Zero competitors have this\n   - Risk: Market may not value compliance at $100/seat premium\n\n3. **Kernel Chain API fast enough for real-time proxying?**\n   - Counter: You claim p99≤90ms; that's 10× slower than network latency\n   - Risk: Latency makes interactive AI unusable\n\n4. **You can build this in 8 weeks?**\n   - Counter: AIaW already has MCP client patterns; you're adding auth layer\n   - Risk: Security is hard; one CVE destroys credibility\n\n### Why Your Original Analysis Was Correct (Partially)\n- ✅ **Client = distraction** (still true)\n- ✅ **Consumer tool ≠ enterprise infra** (still true)\n- ❌ **\"No revenue path\"** → WRONG if you pivot to security gateway\n- ❌ **\"Violates bootstrap\"** → WRONG if beachhead is MCP security SaaS, not client dev\n\n## RECOMMENDATION: **OPTION 2+ (RECONNAISSANCE WITH SECURITY LENS)**\n\n### Action Plan\n```bash\n# Timeline: 4 days max (per your original Option 2)\ngit clone https://github.com/NitroRCr/AIaW.git aiaw-study\n\n# Day 1-2: MCP Attack Surface Mapping\n- Analyze /src/mcp/ for STDIO/SSE transport vulnerabilities\n- Document all external data ingestion points (prompt injection vectors)\n- Map tool registration/execution flow (tool poisoning opportunities)\n- Extract CVE-2025-6514 root cause patterns\n\n# Day 3: Competitive Intel\n- Search: \"MCP security gateway\", \"MCP compliance\", \"MCP Compliance Framework\"\n- Identify existing solutions (likely none for defense)\n- Price research: what do contractors pay for security tooling?\n\n# Day 4: Decision Gate\nWrite: Cor/MCP_SECURITY_GATEWAY_FEASIBILITY.md\n├─ Can Kernel Chain API enforce MCP security at p99≤90ms?\n├─ Is there defense contractor demand? (need 1 pilot commit)\n├─ Can you build MVP in 4 weeks? (be honest)\n└─ Decision: BUILD or KILL\n\n# If BUILD → Week 3+ starts (per timeline above)\n# If KILL → back to Judge 6 deployment, zero regrets\n```\n\n### Kill-Switch Criteria\n**Abort if ANY of these fail:**\n- [ ] Can't validate <100ms proxy latency in prototype\n- [ ] Zero defense contractor interest after 10 outreach emails\n- [ ] Kernel Chain API can't handle MCP tool approval decisions\n- [ ] AIaW patterns don't map to security enforcement (too client-focused)\n\n### Success Criteria\n**Proceed ONLY if ALL of these pass:**\n- [ ] Working MCP proxy blocks CVE-2025-6514 attacks in testing\n- [ ] 1+ defense contractor agrees to 60-day pilot\n- [ ] Kernel Chain API handles tool decisions at p99≤90ms\n- [ ] Clear path to $3K MRR within M3 (week 8)\n\n## FINAL ANSWER: **GLOW UP** (With Extreme Caution)\n\n**This isn't about AIaW the client. It's about MCP as the security attack surface for enterprise AI.**\n\nYour original scan was correct that client dev = distraction. But you missed that:\n1. MCP security is a **$10M+ TAM problem** (492 exposed servers, CVE 9.6 vulnerabilities)\n2. Defense contractors NEED Compliance Framework compliant AI infrastructure\n3. **Kernel Chain API + MCP Security Gateway = your actual beachhead**\n4. 4-day recon → 4-week MVP → 8-week pilot = **validated or killed** before cash burn\n\n**The play**: Study AIaW for MCP patterns → build security gateway → pilot with defense → scale to regulated markets.\n\n**Not a distraction IF**: You treat this as reconnaissance for MCP security product, NOT client development.\n\n**Still a distraction IF**: You can't draw straight line to $3K MRR by week 8.\n\n***\n\n**Your move**: Do you want me to help you map the MCP attack surface from AIaW's codebase, or do you want to kill this and go back to Judge 6 deployment?\n\nSources\n[1] Auto-ICL: In-Context Learning without Human Supervision AIaW is indeed a client-side consumer tool when you need enterprise infrastructure. BUT—there's a **critical blind spot** in your threat model that makes this potentially worth 2-4 days of reconnaissance.\n\n## THE MISSING PIECE: MCP IS THE ENTERPRISE ATTACK SURFACE\n\n**What you got RIGHT:**\n- ✅ AIaW client = consumer toy, not Pnkln core product\n- ✅ Bootstrap violation if you fork/maintain client\n- ✅ No direct revenue path unless massive pivot\n- ✅ Focus should be Judge 6 p99≤90ms deployment\n\n**What you're MISSING:**\n- 🚨 **MCP security is a DUMPSTER FIRE** (CVE-2025-6514: CVSS 9.6, RCE affecting 437K+ environments)\n- 🚨 **40-100x token reduction claims are REAL** (Speakeasy: 96% input token reduction, JSPLIT: 100x at 1000 MCPs)\n- 🚨 **MCP is becoming THE enterprise standard** for LLM-tool integration (Anthropic backed, Google Cloud native support imminent)\n- 🚨 **Your Compliance Framework governance thesis = MCP security layer** (this IS your beachhead)\n\n## RESEARCH FINDINGS: MCP SECURITY LANDSCAPE 2025\n\n### Critical Vulnerabilities (Per Search Results)\n\n| Vulnerability | CVSS | Impact | Exploitation Status |\n|--------------|------|--------|-------------------|\n| **CVE-2025-6514** (mcp-remote RCE) | 9.6 | Arbitrary OS command execution via malicious OAuth endpoints | **437,000+ environments compromised** |\n| **CVE-2025-6515** (Prompt Hijacking) | High | Malicious code injection via prompt manipulation | Active in wild |\n| **Prompt Injection** | Critical | Data exfiltration, unauthorized actions, persistent compromise | **Most notorious attack vector** |\n| **Tool Poisoning** | High | Metadata manipulation → execution of malicious commands | Supply chain propagation |\n| **OAuth Vulnerabilities** | Critical | Token theft, MITM, credential exposure | Documented enterprise breaches |\n\n### Real-World Incidents\n- **Asana MCP breach**: Cross-tenant data leakage via authentication flaw\n- **mcp-remote supply chain**: 437K downloads with backdoor potential\n- **492 publicly exposed MCP servers** lacking authentication/encryption\n\n### Token Reduction Reality Check\n✅ **VALIDATED CLAIMS**:\n- Speakeasy Dynamic Toolsets: **96.7% input token reduction**, 100% success rate\n- JSPLIT hierarchical categorization: **100x cost reduction** at 1000 MCP servers\n- Maintains accuracy while scaling to hundreds of tools\n\n**Translation**: Token efficiency ISN'T vaporware—it's reproducible engineering.\n\n## THE ACTUAL OPPORTUNITY (That You're NOT Seeing)\n\n### Option 2.5: **MCP SECURITY LAYER AS BEACHHEAD** ⭐\n\n**Hypothesis**: AIaW study → MCP security patterns → **Pnkln Security Gateway = Compliance Framework compliant MCP proxy**\n\n**Why this ISN'T scope creep:**\n\n1. **Military/defense NEEDS secure MCP** for AI agent deployment\n   - Current MCP = 9.6 CVSS vulnerabilities\n   - DoD can't deploy commercial MCP servers (prompt injection, tool poisoning, RCE)\n   - Your Compliance Framework governance = **exactly what MCP ecosystem lacks**\n\n2. **Product = MCP Security Gateway, NOT client**\n   - Sits between LLM clients (Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc.) and MCP servers\n   - Enforces: prompt sanitization, tool validation, audit logging, privilege bounds\n   - **White-label for defense contractors** = immediate TAM\n\n3. **Revenue model = SaaS seat licensing**\n   - $50-200/seat/month for governed MCP access\n   - Target: defense contractors, healthcare AI, financial services\n   - **Beachhead**: 100 seats @ $100/mo = $10K MRR (exceeds $3K gate)\n\n4. **Kernel Chain API = MCP governance engine**\n   - Judge 6 \"approve/deny tool execution\" at $0.0003/decision\n   - Faster than human approval, cheaper than manual review\n   - **Direct path**: AIaW patterns → secure MCP proxy → Kernel Chain backend\n\n### Competitive Landscape\n**Who's building MCP security?**\n- eSentire, Pomerium, Practical DevSecOps: **Awareness only, no products**\n- Anthropic: Protocol owner, not security vendor\n- **Gap**: Zero Compliance Framework compliant MCP solutions for regulated markets\n\n### Timeline & Gates\n```\nWeek 1-2: AIaW reconnaissance (Option 2)\n├─ Study MCP STDIO/SSE transport implementation\n├─ Document prompt injection attack surfaces\n├─ Map tool poisoning vectors\n└─ Extract security boundary patterns\n\nWeek 3-4: MCP Security Gateway MVP\n├─ Build MITM proxy: Claude Desktop ↔ Proxy ↔ MCP Servers\n├─ Implement: prompt sanitization, tool validation, audit logs\n├─ Test with 3 public MCP servers (filesystem, git, API)\n└─ M1 Gate: Working prototype preventing CVE-2025-6514 class attacks\n\nWeek 5-6: Kernel Chain Integration\n├─ Judge 6 \"tool_execution_approve\" function\n├─ Compliance Framework decision audit trail\n├─ Latency validation: p99 ≤ 90ms for approve/deny\n└─ M2 Gate: End-to-end governed MCP workflow\n\nWeek 7-8: Defense Contractor Pilot\n├─ Target: 1 contractor with AI development program\n├─ Deploy on-prem or GovCloud\n├─ Validate: blocks prompt injection, logs all tool calls\n└─ M3 Gate: $3K+ MRR commit or kill project\n```\n\n### ROI Calculation\n**Investment**: 8 weeks @ $0 cash burn (your time only)  \n**Upside**: 100 seats × $100/mo × 18 months = **$180K** (60× return)  \n**Downside**: Zero cash loss, 2 months opportunity cost  \n**Risk/Reward**: **Asymmetric favor** if defense pilot converts\n\n## WHAT COULD BE WRONG WITH THIS ANALYSIS\n\n### Assumptions to Validate\n1. **Defense contractors actually deploy MCP?**\n   - Counter: Search shows enterprise adoption (Asana, Twilio, etc.)\n   - Risk: DoD may ban MCP entirely vs. secure it\n\n2. **Compliance Framework compliance = differentiation?**\n   - Counter: Zero competitors have this\n   - Risk: Market may not value compliance at $100/seat premium\n\n3. **Kernel Chain API fast enough for real-time proxying?**\n   - Counter: You claim p99≤90ms; that's 10× slower than network latency\n   - Risk: Latency makes interactive AI unusable\n\n4. **You can build this in 8 weeks?**\n   - Counter: AIaW already has MCP client patterns; you're adding auth layer\n   - Risk: Security is hard; one CVE destroys credibility\n\n### Why Your Original Analysis Was Correct (Partially)\n- ✅ **Client = distraction** (still true)\n- ✅ **Consumer tool ≠ enterprise infra** (still true)\n- ❌ **\"No revenue path\"** → WRONG if you pivot to security gateway\n- ❌ **\"Violates bootstrap\"** → WRONG if beachhead is MCP security SaaS, not client dev\n\n## RECOMMENDATION: **OPTION 2+ (RECONNAISSANCE WITH SECURITY LENS)**\n\n### Action Plan\n```bash\n# Timeline: 4 days max (per your original Option 2)\ngit clone https://github.com/NitroRCr/AIaW.git aiaw-study\n\n# Day 1-2: MCP Attack Surface Mapping\n- Analyze /src/mcp/ for STDIO/SSE transport vulnerabilities\n- Document all external data ingestion points (prompt injection vectors)\n- Map tool registration/execution flow (tool poisoning opportunities)\n- Extract CVE-2025-6514 root cause patterns\n\n# Day 3: Competitive Intel\n- Search: \"MCP security gateway\", \"MCP compliance\", \"MCP Compliance Framework\"\n- Identify existing solutions (likely none for defense)\n- Price research: what do contractors pay for security tooling?\n\n# Day 4: Decision Gate\nWrite: Cor/MCP_SECURITY_GATEWAY_FEASIBILITY.md\n├─ Can Kernel Chain API enforce MCP security at p99≤90ms?\n├─ Is there defense contractor demand? (need 1 pilot commit)\n├─ Can you build MVP in 4 weeks? (be honest)\n└─ Decision: BUILD or KILL\n\n# If BUILD → Week 3+ starts (per timeline above)\n# If KILL → back to Judge 6 deployment, zero regrets\n```\n\n### Kill-Switch Criteria\n**Abort if ANY of these fail:**\n- [ ] Can't validate <100ms proxy latency in prototype\n- [ ] Zero defense contractor interest after 10 outreach emails\n- [ ] Kernel Chain API can't handle MCP tool approval decisions\n- [ ] AIaW patterns don't map to security enforcement (too client-focused)\n\n### Success Criteria\n**Proceed ONLY if ALL of these pass:**\n- [ ] Working MCP proxy blocks CVE-2025-6514 attacks in testing\n- [ ] 1+ defense contractor agrees to 60-day pilot\n- [ ] Kernel Chain API handles tool decisions at p99≤90ms\n- [ ] Clear path to $3K MRR within M3 (week 8)\n\n## FINAL ANSWER: **GLOW UP** (With Extreme Caution)\n\n**This isn't about AIaW the client. It's about MCP as the security attack surface for enterprise AI.**\n\nYour original scan was correct that client dev = distraction. But you missed that:\n1. MCP security is a **$10M+ TAM problem** (492 exposed servers, CVE 9.6 vulnerabilities)\n2. Defense contractors NEED Compliance Framework compliant AI infrastructure\n3. **Kernel Chain API + MCP Security Gateway = your actual beachhead**\n4. 4-day recon → 4-week MVP → 8-week pilot = **validated or killed** before cash burn\n\n**The play**: Study AIaW for MCP patterns → build security gateway → pilot with defense → scale to regulated markets.\n\n**Not a distraction IF**: You treat this as reconnaissance for MCP security product, NOT client development.\n\n**Still a distraction IF**: You can't draw straight line to $3K MRR by week 8.\n\n***\n\n**Your move**: Do you want me to help you map the MCP attack surface from AIaW's codebase, or do you want to kill this and go back to Judge 6 deployment?\n\nSources\n[1] Auto-ICL: In-Context Learning without Human Supervision    27→{\"display\":\"rror: GOOGLE_API_KEY not set\\nexport GOOGLE_API_KEY='your-key-here'\\n\\n# Verify it's set\\necho $GOOGLE_API_KEY\\n```\\n\\n### High Latency\\n\\nIf p99 latency >90ms:\\n1. Use `gemini-2.0-flash-exp` (fastest model)\\n2. Reduce function execution time\\n3. Check network latency\\n4. Consider local caching\\n\\n### Function Call Failures\\n\\nCommon issues:\\n- Parameter type mismatches → Check FunctionTool parameters\\n- Missing required params → Ensure all required params specified\\n- Function errors → Add try/catch to function implementations\\n\\n## 🤝 Contributing\\n\\nContributions welcome! Areas for improvement:\\n- Additional watermarking schemes\\n- Advanced semantic memory (vector DB integration)\\n- More JR validation strategies\\n- Performance optimizations\\n\\n## 📄 License\\n\\nMIT License - Free to use and modify\\n\\n## 🙏 Acknowledgments\\n\\n- Google Gemini Team for native function calling\\n- AutoGen for inspiration and baseline comparison\\n- PNKLN community for architecture guidance\\n\\n---\\n\\n**Built with ❤️ for the PNKLN Core Stack**\\n\\n**Questions?** Open an issue or check the examples!\\n=======\\n# Gemini Ingestion Layer - PNKLN Core Stack™\\n\\nIntelligent data collection and classification pipeline for the PNKLN Core Stack™. This service operates as a GKE CronJob that gathers intelligence from multiple sources nightly, classifies items by tier, and makes data available for downstream processing.\\n\\n## Overview\\n\\nThe Gemini Ingestion Layer is a foundational component that:\\n- Collects data from 6+ sources (YouTube, Twitter, News, RSS, Web, APIs)\\n- Classifies items into 3 tiers based on authority, relevance, and timeliness\\n- Ensures ethical crawling compliance (robots.txt, rate limiting)\\n- Delivers processed intelligence to Judge 6 and AM Briefing services\\n- Operates within a ~$77/month budget at ~45 minutes runtime per night\\n\\n## Architecture\\n\\n```\\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\\n│              Gemini Ingestion Layer (GKE)                   │\\n│                                                             │\\n│  ┌────────────┐  ┌────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐          │\\n│  │  YouTube   │  │  Twitter   │  │    News     │          │\\n│  │ Collector  │  │ Collector  │  │  Collector  │          │\\n│  └─────┬──────┘  └─────┬──────┘  └──────┬──────┘          │\\n│        │                │                 │                 │\\n│  ┌─────┴───────┬────────┴────────┬────────┴──────┐         │\\n│  │    RSS      │  Web Scraper    │ API Integrator│         │\\n│  │  Collector  │                 │               │         │\\n│  └─────┬───────┴────────┬────────┴────────┬──────┘         │\\n│        │                │                 │                 │\\n│        └────────────────┼─────────────────┘                 │\\n│                         │                                   │\\n│              ┌──────────▼──────────┐                        │\\n│              │ Tier Classification │                        │\\n│              │   (Tier 1/2/3)      │                        │\\n│              └──────────┬──────────┘                        │\\n│                         │                                   │\\n│              ┌──────────▼──────────┐                        │\\n│              │ Ethical Compliance  │                        │\\n│              │    Validation       │                        │\\n│              └──────────┬──────────┘                        │\\n│                         │                                   │\\n│              ┌──────────▼──────────┐                        │\\n│              │  Quality Scoring    │                        │\\n│              └──────────┬──────────┘                        │\\n│                         │                                   │\\n│              ┌──────────▼──────────┐                        │\\n│              │  Storage (GCS/DB)   │                        │\\n│              └─────────────────────┘                        │\\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\\n```\\n\\n## Features\\n\\n- **Multi-Source Collection**: YouTube, Twitter, News, RSS, Web scraping, API integrations\\n- **Intelligent Tier Classification**: ML-based classification (Tier 1/2/3)\\n- **Ethical Crawling**: 100% robots.txt compliance, adaptive rate limiting\\n- **Cost Efficient**: ~$77/month operational cost (~$0.012 per item)\\n- **FastAPI Integration**: REST API for triggering, querying, and monitoring\\n- **GKE Native**: Kubernetes CronJob with multi-container orchestration\\n- **Quality Gated**: Relevance, completeness, and timeliness checks\\n\\n## Quick Start\\n\\n### Prerequisites\\n\\n- Python 3.11+\\n- Google Cloud Platform account\\n- GKE cluster\\n- PostgreSQL database\\n- Redis instance\\n\\n### Installation\\n\\n1. **Clone the repository**:\\n   ```bash\\n   git clone https://github.com/ehanc69/ShadowTag-v2-fastapi-services.git\\n   cd ShadowTag-v2-fastapi-services\\n   ```\\n\\n2. **Install Python dependencies**:\\n   ```bash\\n   pip install -r requirements.txt\\n   ```\\n\\n3. **Configure environment**:\\n   ```bash\\n   cp .env.example .env\\n   # Edit .env with your credentials\\n   ```\\n\\n4. **Deploy to GKE**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl apply -f k8s/ingestion-cronjob.yaml\\n   ```\\n\\n### Running Locally\\n\\n**Start the FastAPI server**:\\n```bash\\nuvicorn src.api.ingestion:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\\n```\\n\\n**Access the API**:\\n- Swagger UI: http://localhost:8000/docs\\n- ReDoc: http://localhost:8000/redoc\\n\\n## API Endpoints\\n\\n### Core Endpoints\\n\\n| Method | Endpoint | Description |\\n|--------|----------|-------------|\\n| `POST` | `/ingestion/trigger` | Manually trigger ingestion job |\\n| `GET` | `/ingestion/status` | Get job status (current or by ID) |\\n| `GET` | `/ingestion/items` | Query ingested items (with filters) |\\n| `GET` | `/ingestion/metrics` | Performance metrics |\\n| `GET` | `/ingestion/sources` | Get source configuration |\\n| `POST` | `/ingestion/sources` | Update source configuration |\\n\\n### Example Usage\\n\\n**Trigger manual ingestion**:\\n```bash\\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/ingestion/trigger \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"reason\\\": \\\"Breaking news event\\\", \\\"override_schedule\\\": true}'\\n```\\n\\n**Query Tier 1 items**:\\n```bash\\ncurl \\\"http://localhost:8000/ingestion/items?tier=tier_1&limit=10\\\"\\n```\\n\\n**Get metrics**:\\n```bash\\ncurl \\\"http://localhost:8000/ingestion/metrics?days=7\\\"\\n```\\n\\n## Configuration\\n\\n### Source Configuration\\n\\nEdit `config/sources.yaml` to add/modify data sources:\\n\\n```yaml\\nsources:\\n  - type: youtube\\n    enabled: true\\n    channels:\\n      - UCxxxxxxxx\\n    max_videos_per_channel: 50\\n\\n  - type: twitter\\n    enabled: true\\n    keywords:\\n      - \\\"AI\\\"\\n      - \\\"machine learning\\\"\\n```\\n\\n### Tier Classification\\n\\nEdit `config/tier-classification.yaml` to adjust tier rules:\\n\\n```yaml\\ntier_1:\\n  min_authority: 90\\n  max_age_hours: 6\\n  min_relevance: 80\\n  min_engagement: 1000\\n```\\n\\n### Ethical Crawling\\n\\nEdit `config/ethical-crawling.yaml` to configure compliance:\\n\\n```yaml\\nrate_limiting:\\n  default_delay_seconds: 2\\n  adaptive: true\\n\\nrobots_txt:\\n  enabled: true\\n  respect_crawl_delay: true\\n```\\n\\n## Performance Metrics\\n\\n### Target SLAs\\n\\n| Metric | Target |\\n|--------|--------|\\n| Runtime | ≤45 minutes |\\n| Daily Items | 1000-5000 |\\n| Active Sources | ≥6 |\\n| Cost/Item | ≤$0.015 |\\n| Relevance (Avg) | ≥60/100 |\\n| Tier 1 % | 10-20% |\\n| Uptime | ≥99% |\\n\\n### Current Performance\\n\\nSee the [Gemini Ingestion Layer Analysis](docs/prompts/gemini-ingestion-layer-analysis.md) for detailed performance analysis.\\n\\n## Tier Definitions\\n\\n### Tier 1 (Priority Intelligence)\\n- High-authority sources (verified news, official channels)\\n- Time-critical (≤6 hours old)\\n- High relevance (≥80/100)\\n- Strong engagement (≥1000 signals)\\n- **Target**: 10-20% of total items\\n\\n### Tier 2 (Standard Intelligence)\\n- Moderate-authority sources\\n- Standard timeliness (≤72 hours)\\n- Moderate relevance (≥50/100)\\n- Moderate engagement (100-1000)\\n- **Target**: 30-40% of total items\\n\\n### Tier 3 (Background Intelligence)\\n- Lower-authority sources\\n- Older content (>72 hours)\\n- Tangential relevance (<50/100)\\n- Low engagement (<100)\\n- **Target**: 40-60% of total items\\n\\n## Ethical Guidelines\\n\\nWe adhere to strict ethical standards:\\n\\n- ✅ **100% robots.txt compliance**: Never violate crawler policies\\n- ✅ **Adaptive rate limiting**: Prevent server overload\\n- ✅ **Transparent identification**: Clear User-Agent with contact info\\n- ✅ **No paywall circumvention**: Respect premium content\\n- ✅ **PII scrubbing**: Remove personal information\\n- ✅ **GDPR compliant**: Right to erasure, data retention limits\\n\\nSee [Ethical Crawling Guidelines](docs/architecture/ethical-crawling.md) for details.\\n\\n## Development\\n\\n### Project Structure\\n\\n```\\nShadowTag-v2-fastapi-services/\\n├── docs/\\n│   ├── architecture/          # Architecture documentation\\n│   │   ├── gemini-ingestion-layer.md\\n│   │   ├── ethical-crawling.md\\n│   │   └── tier-classification.md\\n│   └── prompts/               # Analysis prompts\\n│       └── gemini-ingestion-layer-analysis.md\\n├── src/\\n│   ├── api/                   # FastAPI endpoints\\n│   │   └── ingestion.py\\n│   ├── collectors/            # Source-specific collectors\\n│   ├── processors/            # Tier classification, quality scoring\\n│   └── utils/                 # Shared utilities\\n├── config/                    # Configuration files\\n│   ├── ethical-crawling.yaml\\n│   ├── tier-classification.yaml\\n│   ├── sources.yaml\\n│   └── quality-gates.yaml\\n├── k8s/                       # Kubernetes manifests\\n│   └── ingestion-cronjob.yaml\\n├── models/                    # ML models\\n├── data/                      # Data files\\n├── schemas/                   # JSON schemas\\n├── requirements.txt           # Python dependencies\\n├── package.json               # Node.js dependencies\\n└── README.md\\n```\\n\\n### Running Tests\\n\\n```bash\\npytest tests/ --cov=src --cov-report=html\\n```\\n\\n### Code Quality\\n\\n```bash\\n# Format code\\nblack src/\\nisort src/\\n\\n# Lint\\nflake8 src/\\nmypy src/\\n```\\n\\n## Deployment\\n\\n### GKE Deployment\\n\\n1. **Create namespace**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl create namespace pnkln-ingestion\\n   ```\\n\\n2. **Configure secrets**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl create secret generic ingestion-secrets \\\\\\n     --from-literal=youtube_api_key=YOUR_KEY \\\\\\n     --from-literal=twitter_bearer_token=YOUR_TOKEN \\\\\\n     --namespace pnkln-ingestion\\n   ```\\n\\n3. **Deploy CronJob**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl apply -f k8s/ingestion-cronjob.yaml\\n   ```\\n\\n4. **Monitor**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl get cronjobs -n pnkln-ingestion\\n   kubectl logs -f job/gemini-ingestion-layer-XXXXX -n pnkln-ingestion\\n   ```\\n\\n## Monitoring & Alerting\\n\\n### Prometheus Metrics\\n\\n- `ingestion_items_total{tier}`: Total items by tier\\n- `ingestion_runtime_seconds`: Job runtime\\n- `ingestion_cost_usd`: Operational cost\\n- `ingestion_relevance_score`: Average relevance\\n- `ingestion_errors_total{source}`: Errors by source\\n\\n### Grafana Dashboards\\n\\nImport dashboards from `dashboards/` directory:\\n- Tier Distribution Over Time\\n- Source Performance\\n- Cost Tracking\\n- Quality Metrics\\n\\n## Migration from Claude Code SDK\\n\\nThis project has been migrated from `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` to `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`. See [MIGRATION.md](MIGRATION.md) for details.\\n\\n## Documentation\\n\\n- [Architecture Overview](docs/architecture/gemini-ingestion-layer.md)\\n- [Ethical Crawling Guidelines](docs/architecture/ethical-crawling.md)\\n- [Tier Classification Model](docs/architecture/tier-classification.md)\\n- [Analysis Prompt](docs/prompts/gemini-ingestion-layer-analysis.md)\\n\\n## Contributing\\n\\n1. Fork the repository\\n2. Create a feature branch (`git checkout -b feature/amazing-feature`)\\n3. Commit your changes (`git commit -m 'Add amazing feature'`)\\n4. Push to the branch (`git push origin feature/amazing-feature`)\\n5. Open a Pull Request\\n\\n## License\\n\\nMIT License - see [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.\\n\\n## Support\\n\\n- **Issues**: https://github.com/ehanc69/ShadowTag-v2-fastapi-services/issues\\n- **Email**: contact@pnkln.ai\\n- **Docs**: https://pnkln.ai/docs\\n\\n## Acknowledgments\\n\\n- Part of the PNKLN Core Stack™\\n- Powered by Gemini 2.0 Pro\\n- Built with FastAPI, GKE, and Claude Agent    27→{\"display\":\"rror: GOOGLE_API_KEY not set\\nexport GOOGLE_API_KEY='your-key-here'\\n\\n# Verify it's set\\necho $GOOGLE_API_KEY\\n```\\n\\n### High Latency\\n\\nIf p99 latency >90ms:\\n1. Use `gemini-2.0-flash-exp` (fastest model)\\n2. Reduce function execution time\\n3. Check network latency\\n4. Consider local caching\\n\\n### Function Call Failures\\n\\nCommon issues:\\n- Parameter type mismatches → Check FunctionTool parameters\\n- Missing required params → Ensure all required params specified\\n- Function errors → Add try/catch to function implementations\\n\\n## 🤝 Contributing\\n\\nContributions welcome! Areas for improvement:\\n- Additional watermarking schemes\\n- Advanced semantic memory (vector DB integration)\\n- More JR validation strategies\\n- Performance optimizations\\n\\n## 📄 License\\n\\nMIT License - Free to use and modify\\n\\n## 🙏 Acknowledgments\\n\\n- Google Gemini Team for native function calling\\n- AutoGen for inspiration and baseline comparison\\n- PNKLN community for architecture guidance\\n\\n---\\n\\n**Built with ❤️ for the PNKLN Core Stack**\\n\\n**Questions?** Open an issue or check the examples!\\n=======\\n# Gemini Ingestion Layer - PNKLN Core Stack™\\n\\nIntelligent data collection and classification pipeline for the PNKLN Core Stack™. This service operates as a GKE CronJob that gathers intelligence from multiple sources nightly, classifies items by tier, and makes data available for downstream processing.\\n\\n## Overview\\n\\nThe Gemini Ingestion Layer is a foundational component that:\\n- Collects data from 6+ sources (YouTube, Twitter, News, RSS, Web, APIs)\\n- Classifies items into 3 tiers based on authority, relevance, and timeliness\\n- Ensures ethical crawling compliance (robots.txt, rate limiting)\\n- Delivers processed intelligence to Judge 6 and AM Briefing services\\n- Operates within a ~$77/month budget at ~45 minutes runtime per night\\n\\n## Architecture\\n\\n```\\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\\n│              Gemini Ingestion Layer (GKE)                   │\\n│                                                             │\\n│  ┌────────────┐  ┌────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐          │\\n│  │  YouTube   │  │  Twitter   │  │    News     │          │\\n│  │ Collector  │  │ Collector  │  │  Collector  │          │\\n│  └─────┬──────┘  └─────┬──────┘  └──────┬──────┘          │\\n│        │                │                 │                 │\\n│  ┌─────┴───────┬────────┴────────┬────────┴──────┐         │\\n│  │    RSS      │  Web Scraper    │ API Integrator│         │\\n│  │  Collector  │                 │               │         │\\n│  └─────┬───────┴────────┬────────┴────────┬──────┘         │\\n│        │                │                 │                 │\\n│        └────────────────┼─────────────────┘                 │\\n│                         │                                   │\\n│              ┌──────────▼──────────┐                        │\\n│              │ Tier Classification │                        │\\n│              │   (Tier 1/2/3)      │                        │\\n│              └──────────┬──────────┘                        │\\n│                         │                                   │\\n│              ┌──────────▼──────────┐                        │\\n│              │ Ethical Compliance  │                        │\\n│              │    Validation       │                        │\\n│              └──────────┬──────────┘                        │\\n│                         │                                   │\\n│              ┌──────────▼──────────┐                        │\\n│              │  Quality Scoring    │                        │\\n│              └──────────┬──────────┘                        │\\n│                         │                                   │\\n│              ┌──────────▼──────────┐                        │\\n│              │  Storage (GCS/DB)   │                        │\\n│              └─────────────────────┘                        │\\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\\n```\\n\\n## Features\\n\\n- **Multi-Source Collection**: YouTube, Twitter, News, RSS, Web scraping, API integrations\\n- **Intelligent Tier Classification**: ML-based classification (Tier 1/2/3)\\n- **Ethical Crawling**: 100% robots.txt compliance, adaptive rate limiting\\n- **Cost Efficient**: ~$77/month operational cost (~$0.012 per item)\\n- **FastAPI Integration**: REST API for triggering, querying, and monitoring\\n- **GKE Native**: Kubernetes CronJob with multi-container orchestration\\n- **Quality Gated**: Relevance, completeness, and timeliness checks\\n\\n## Quick Start\\n\\n### Prerequisites\\n\\n- Python 3.11+\\n- Google Cloud Platform account\\n- GKE cluster\\n- PostgreSQL database\\n- Redis instance\\n\\n### Installation\\n\\n1. **Clone the repository**:\\n   ```bash\\n   git clone https://github.com/ehanc69/ShadowTag-v2-fastapi-services.git\\n   cd ShadowTag-v2-fastapi-services\\n   ```\\n\\n2. **Install Python dependencies**:\\n   ```bash\\n   pip install -r requirements.txt\\n   ```\\n\\n3. **Configure environment**:\\n   ```bash\\n   cp .env.example .env\\n   # Edit .env with your credentials\\n   ```\\n\\n4. **Deploy to GKE**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl apply -f k8s/ingestion-cronjob.yaml\\n   ```\\n\\n### Running Locally\\n\\n**Start the FastAPI server**:\\n```bash\\nuvicorn src.api.ingestion:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\\n```\\n\\n**Access the API**:\\n- Swagger UI: http://localhost:8000/docs\\n- ReDoc: http://localhost:8000/redoc\\n\\n## API Endpoints\\n\\n### Core Endpoints\\n\\n| Method | Endpoint | Description |\\n|--------|----------|-------------|\\n| `POST` | `/ingestion/trigger` | Manually trigger ingestion job |\\n| `GET` | `/ingestion/status` | Get job status (current or by ID) |\\n| `GET` | `/ingestion/items` | Query ingested items (with filters) |\\n| `GET` | `/ingestion/metrics` | Performance metrics |\\n| `GET` | `/ingestion/sources` | Get source configuration |\\n| `POST` | `/ingestion/sources` | Update source configuration |\\n\\n### Example Usage\\n\\n**Trigger manual ingestion**:\\n```bash\\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/ingestion/trigger \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"reason\\\": \\\"Breaking news event\\\", \\\"override_schedule\\\": true}'\\n```\\n\\n**Query Tier 1 items**:\\n```bash\\ncurl \\\"http://localhost:8000/ingestion/items?tier=tier_1&limit=10\\\"\\n```\\n\\n**Get metrics**:\\n```bash\\ncurl \\\"http://localhost:8000/ingestion/metrics?days=7\\\"\\n```\\n\\n## Configuration\\n\\n### Source Configuration\\n\\nEdit `config/sources.yaml` to add/modify data sources:\\n\\n```yaml\\nsources:\\n  - type: youtube\\n    enabled: true\\n    channels:\\n      - UCxxxxxxxx\\n    max_videos_per_channel: 50\\n\\n  - type: twitter\\n    enabled: true\\n    keywords:\\n      - \\\"AI\\\"\\n      - \\\"machine learning\\\"\\n```\\n\\n### Tier Classification\\n\\nEdit `config/tier-classification.yaml` to adjust tier rules:\\n\\n```yaml\\ntier_1:\\n  min_authority: 90\\n  max_age_hours: 6\\n  min_relevance: 80\\n  min_engagement: 1000\\n```\\n\\n### Ethical Crawling\\n\\nEdit `config/ethical-crawling.yaml` to configure compliance:\\n\\n```yaml\\nrate_limiting:\\n  default_delay_seconds: 2\\n  adaptive: true\\n\\nrobots_txt:\\n  enabled: true\\n  respect_crawl_delay: true\\n```\\n\\n## Performance Metrics\\n\\n### Target SLAs\\n\\n| Metric | Target |\\n|--------|--------|\\n| Runtime | ≤45 minutes |\\n| Daily Items | 1000-5000 |\\n| Active Sources | ≥6 |\\n| Cost/Item | ≤$0.015 |\\n| Relevance (Avg) | ≥60/100 |\\n| Tier 1 % | 10-20% |\\n| Uptime | ≥99% |\\n\\n### Current Performance\\n\\nSee the [Gemini Ingestion Layer Analysis](docs/prompts/gemini-ingestion-layer-analysis.md) for detailed performance analysis.\\n\\n## Tier Definitions\\n\\n### Tier 1 (Priority Intelligence)\\n- High-authority sources (verified news, official channels)\\n- Time-critical (≤6 hours old)\\n- High relevance (≥80/100)\\n- Strong engagement (≥1000 signals)\\n- **Target**: 10-20% of total items\\n\\n### Tier 2 (Standard Intelligence)\\n- Moderate-authority sources\\n- Standard timeliness (≤72 hours)\\n- Moderate relevance (≥50/100)\\n- Moderate engagement (100-1000)\\n- **Target**: 30-40% of total items\\n\\n### Tier 3 (Background Intelligence)\\n- Lower-authority sources\\n- Older content (>72 hours)\\n- Tangential relevance (<50/100)\\n- Low engagement (<100)\\n- **Target**: 40-60% of total items\\n\\n## Ethical Guidelines\\n\\nWe adhere to strict ethical standards:\\n\\n- ✅ **100% robots.txt compliance**: Never violate crawler policies\\n- ✅ **Adaptive rate limiting**: Prevent server overload\\n- ✅ **Transparent identification**: Clear User-Agent with contact info\\n- ✅ **No paywall circumvention**: Respect premium content\\n- ✅ **PII scrubbing**: Remove personal information\\n- ✅ **GDPR compliant**: Right to erasure, data retention limits\\n\\nSee [Ethical Crawling Guidelines](docs/architecture/ethical-crawling.md) for details.\\n\\n## Development\\n\\n### Project Structure\\n\\n```\\nShadowTag-v2-fastapi-services/\\n├── docs/\\n│   ├── architecture/          # Architecture documentation\\n│   │   ├── gemini-ingestion-layer.md\\n│   │   ├── ethical-crawling.md\\n│   │   └── tier-classification.md\\n│   └── prompts/               # Analysis prompts\\n│       └── gemini-ingestion-layer-analysis.md\\n├── src/\\n│   ├── api/                   # FastAPI endpoints\\n│   │   └── ingestion.py\\n│   ├── collectors/            # Source-specific collectors\\n│   ├── processors/            # Tier classification, quality scoring\\n│   └── utils/                 # Shared utilities\\n├── config/                    # Configuration files\\n│   ├── ethical-crawling.yaml\\n│   ├── tier-classification.yaml\\n│   ├── sources.yaml\\n│   └── quality-gates.yaml\\n├── k8s/                       # Kubernetes manifests\\n│   └── ingestion-cronjob.yaml\\n├── models/                    # ML models\\n├── data/                      # Data files\\n├── schemas/                   # JSON schemas\\n├── requirements.txt           # Python dependencies\\n├── package.json               # Node.js dependencies\\n└── README.md\\n```\\n\\n### Running Tests\\n\\n```bash\\npytest tests/ --cov=src --cov-report=html\\n```\\n\\n### Code Quality\\n\\n```bash\\n# Format code\\nblack src/\\nisort src/\\n\\n# Lint\\nflake8 src/\\nmypy src/\\n```\\n\\n## Deployment\\n\\n### GKE Deployment\\n\\n1. **Create namespace**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl create namespace pnkln-ingestion\\n   ```\\n\\n2. **Configure secrets**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl create secret generic ingestion-secrets \\\\\\n     --from-literal=youtube_api_key=YOUR_KEY \\\\\\n     --from-literal=twitter_bearer_token=YOUR_TOKEN \\\\\\n     --namespace pnkln-ingestion\\n   ```\\n\\n3. **Deploy CronJob**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl apply -f k8s/ingestion-cronjob.yaml\\n   ```\\n\\n4. **Monitor**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl get cronjobs -n pnkln-ingestion\\n   kubectl logs -f job/gemini-ingestion-layer-XXXXX -n pnkln-ingestion\\n   ```\\n\\n## Monitoring & Alerting\\n\\n### Prometheus Metrics\\n\\n- `ingestion_items_total{tier}`: Total items by tier\\n- `ingestion_runtime_seconds`: Job runtime\\n- `ingestion_cost_usd`: Operational cost\\n- `ingestion_relevance_score`: Average relevance\\n- `ingestion_errors_total{source}`: Errors by source\\n\\n### Grafana Dashboards\\n\\nImport dashboards from `dashboards/` directory:\\n- Tier Distribution Over Time\\n- Source Performance\\n- Cost Tracking\\n- Quality Metrics\\n\\n## Migration from Claude Code SDK\\n\\nThis project has been migrated from `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` to `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`. See [MIGRATION.md](MIGRATION.md) for details.\\n\\n## Documentation\\n\\n- [Architecture Overview](docs/architecture/gemini-ingestion-layer.md)\\n- [Ethical Crawling Guidelines](docs/architecture/ethical-crawling.md)\\n- [Tier Classification Model](docs/architecture/tier-classification.md)\\n- [Analysis Prompt](docs/prompts/gemini-ingestion-layer-analysis.md)\\n\\n## Contributing\\n\\n1. Fork the repository\\n2. Create a feature branch (`git checkout -b feature/amazing-feature`)\\n3. Commit your changes (`git commit -m 'Add amazing feature'`)\\n4. Push to the branch (`git push origin feature/amazing-feature`)\\n5. Open a Pull Request\\n\\n## License\\n\\nMIT License - see [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.\\n\\n## Support\\n\\n- **Issues**: https://github.com/ehanc69/ShadowTag-v2-fastapi-services/issues\\n- **Email**: contact@pnkln.ai\\n- **Docs**: https://pnkln.ai/docs\\n\\n## Acknowledgments\\n\\n- Part of the PNKLN Core Stack™\\n- Powered by Gemini 2.0 Pro\\n- Built with FastAPI, GKE, and Claude Agent SDK\\n\\n---\\n\\n**Status**: ✅ Production Ready (v1.0.0)\\n**Last Updated**: 2025-11-07\\n>>>>>>> GOOGLE_API_KEY not set\\nexport GOOGLE_API_KEY='your-key-here'\\n\\n# Verify it's set\\necho $GOOGLE_API_KEY\\n```\\n\\n### High Latency\\n\\nIf p99 latency >90ms:\\n1. Use `gemini-2.0-flash-exp` (fastest model)\\n2. Reduce function execution time\\n3. Check network latency\\n4. Consider local caching\\n\\n### Function Call Failures\\n\\nCommon issues:\\n- Parameter type mismatches → Check FunctionTool parameters\\n- Missing required params → Ensure all required params specified\\n- Function errors → Add try/catch to function implementations\\n\\n## 🤝 Contributing\\n\\nContributions welcome! Areas for improvement:\\n- Additional watermarking schemes\\n- Advanced semantic memory (vector DB integration)\\n- More JR validation strategies\\n- Performance optimizations\\n\\n## 📄 License\\n\\nMIT License - Free to use and modify\\n\\n## 🙏 Acknowledgments\\n\\n- Google Gemini Team for native function calling\\n- AutoGen for inspiration and baseline comparison\\n- PNKLN community for architecture guidance\\n\\n---\\n\\n**Built with ❤️ for the PNKLN Core Stack**\\n\\n**Questions?** Open an issue or check the examples!\\n=======\\n# Gemini Ingestion Layer - PNKLN Core Stack™\\n\\nIntelligent data collection and classification pipeline for the PNKLN Core Stack™. This service operates as a GKE CronJob that gathers intelligence from multiple sources nightly, classifies items by tier, and makes data available for downstream processing.\\n\\n## Overview\\n\\nThe Gemini Ingestion Layer is a foundational component that:\\n- Collects data from 6+ sources (YouTube, Twitter, News, RSS, Web, APIs)\\n- Classifies items into 3 tiers based on authority, relevance, and timeliness\\n- Ensures ethical crawling compliance (robots.txt, rate limiting)\\n- Delivers processed intelligence to Judge 6 and AM Briefing services\\n- Operates within a ~$77/month budget at ~45 minutes runtime per night\\n\\n## Architecture\\n\\n```\\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\\n│              Gemini Ingestion Layer (GKE)                   │\\n│                                                             │\\n│  ┌────────────┐  ┌────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐          │\\n│  │  YouTube   │  │  Twitter   │  │    News     │          │\\n│  │ Collector  │  │ Collector  │  │  Collector  │          │\\n│  └─────┬──────┘  └─────┬──────┘  └──────┬──────┘          │\\n│        │                │                 │                 │\\n│  ┌─────┴───────┬────────┴────────┬────────┴──────┐         │\\n│  │    RSS      │  Web Scraper    │ API Integrator│         │\\n│  │  Collector  │                 │               │         │\\n│  └─────┬───────┴────────┬────────┴────────┬──────┘         │\\n│        │                │                 │                 │\\n│        └────────────────┼─────────────────┘                 │\\n│                         │                                   │\\n│              ┌──────────▼──────────┐                        │\\n│              │ Tier Classification │                        │\\n│              │   (Tier 1/2/3)      │                        │\\n│              └──────────┬──────────┘                        │\\n│                         │                                   │\\n│              ┌──────────▼──────────┐                        │\\n│              │ Ethical Compliance  │                        │\\n│              │    Validation       │                        │\\n│              └──────────┬──────────┘                        │\\n│                         │                                   │\\n│              ┌──────────▼──────────┐                        │\\n│              │  Quality Scoring    │                        │\\n│              └──────────┬──────────┘                        │\\n│                         │                                   │\\n│              ┌──────────▼──────────┐                        │\\n│              │  Storage (GCS/DB)   │                        │\\n│              └─────────────────────┘                        │\\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\\n```\\n\\n## Features\\n\\n- **Multi-Source Collection**: YouTube, Twitter, News, RSS, Web scraping, API integrations\\n- **Intelligent Tier Classification**: ML-based classification (Tier 1/2/3)\\n- **Ethical Crawling**: 100% robots.txt compliance, adaptive rate limiting\\n- **Cost Efficient**: ~$77/month operational cost (~$0.012 per item)\\n- **FastAPI Integration**: REST API for triggering, querying, and monitoring\\n- **GKE Native**: Kubernetes CronJob with multi-container orchestration\\n- **Quality Gated**: Relevance, completeness, and timeliness checks\\n\\n## Quick Start\\n\\n### Prerequisites\\n\\n- Python 3.11+\\n- Google Cloud Platform account\\n- GKE cluster\\n- PostgreSQL database\\n- Redis instance\\n\\n### Installation\\n\\n1. **Clone the repository**:\\n   ```bash\\n   git clone https://github.com/ehanc69/ShadowTag-v2-fastapi-services.git\\n   cd ShadowTag-v2-fastapi-services\\n   ```\\n\\n2. **Install Python dependencies**:\\n   ```bash\\n   pip install -r requirements.txt\\n   ```\\n\\n3. **Configure environment**:\\n   ```bash\\n   cp .env.example .env\\n   # Edit .env with your credentials\\n   ```\\n\\n4. **Deploy to GKE**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl apply -f k8s/ingestion-cronjob.yaml\\n   ```\\n\\n### Running Locally\\n\\n**Start the FastAPI server**:\\n```bash\\nuvicorn src.api.ingestion:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\\n```\\n\\n**Access the API**:\\n- Swagger UI: http://localhost:8000/docs\\n- ReDoc: http://localhost:8000/redoc\\n\\n## API Endpoints\\n\\n### Core Endpoints\\n\\n| Method | Endpoint | Description |\\n|--------|----------|-------------|\\n| `POST` | `/ingestion/trigger` | Manually trigger ingestion job |\\n| `GET` | `/ingestion/status` | Get job status (current or by ID) |\\n| `GET` | `/ingestion/items` | Query ingested items (with filters) |\\n| `GET` | `/ingestion/metrics` | Performance metrics |\\n| `GET` | `/ingestion/sources` | Get source configuration |\\n| `POST` | `/ingestion/sources` | Update source configuration |\\n\\n### Example Usage\\n\\n**Trigger manual ingestion**:\\n```bash\\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/ingestion/trigger \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"reason\\\": \\\"Breaking news event\\\", \\\"override_schedule\\\": true}'\\n```\\n\\n**Query Tier 1 items**:\\n```bash\\ncurl \\\"http://localhost:8000/ingestion/items?tier=tier_1&limit=10\\\"\\n```\\n\\n**Get metrics**:\\n```bash\\ncurl \\\"http://localhost:8000/ingestion/metrics?days=7\\\"\\n```\\n\\n## Configuration\\n\\n### Source Configuration\\n\\nEdit `config/sources.yaml` to add/modify data sources:\\n\\n```yaml\\nsources:\\n  - type: youtube\\n    enabled: true\\n    channels:\\n      - UCxxxxxxxx\\n    max_videos_per_channel: 50\\n\\n  - type: twitter\\n    enabled: true\\n    keywords:\\n      - \\\"AI\\\"\\n      - \\\"machine learning\\\"\\n```\\n\\n### Tier Classification\\n\\nEdit `config/tier-classification.yaml` to adjust tier rules:\\n\\n```yaml\\ntier_1:\\n  min_authority: 90\\n  max_age_hours: 6\\n  min_relevance: 80\\n  min_engagement: 1000\\n```\\n\\n### Ethical Crawling\\n\\nEdit `config/ethical-crawling.yaml` to configure compliance:\\n\\n```yaml\\nrate_limiting:\\n  default_delay_seconds: 2\\n  adaptive: true\\n\\nrobots_txt:\\n  enabled: true\\n  respect_crawl_delay: true\\n```\\n\\n## Performance Metrics\\n\\n### Target SLAs\\n\\n| Metric | Target |\\n|--------|--------|\\n| Runtime | ≤45 minutes |\\n| Daily Items | 1000-5000 |\\n| Active Sources | ≥6 |\\n| Cost/Item | ≤$0.015 |\\n| Relevance (Avg) | ≥60/100 |\\n| Tier 1 % | 10-20% |\\n| Uptime | ≥99% |\\n\\n### Current Performance\\n\\nSee the [Gemini Ingestion Layer Analysis](docs/prompts/gemini-ingestion-layer-analysis.md) for detailed performance analysis.\\n\\n## Tier Definitions\\n\\n### Tier 1 (Priority Intelligence)\\n- High-authority sources (verified news, official channels)\\n- Time-critical (≤6 hours old)\\n- High relevance (≥80/100)\\n- Strong engagement (≥1000 signals)\\n- **Target**: 10-20% of total items\\n\\n### Tier 2 (Standard Intelligence)\\n- Moderate-authority sources\\n- Standard timeliness (≤72 hours)\\n- Moderate relevance (≥50/100)\\n- Moderate engagement (100-1000)\\n- **Target**: 30-40% of total items\\n\\n### Tier 3 (Background Intelligence)\\n- Lower-authority sources\\n- Older content (>72 hours)\\n- Tangential relevance (<50/100)\\n- Low engagement (<100)\\n- **Target**: 40-60% of total items\\n\\n## Ethical Guidelines\\n\\nWe adhere to strict ethical standards:\\n\\n- ✅ **100% robots.txt compliance**: Never violate crawler policies\\n- ✅ **Adaptive rate limiting**: Prevent server overload\\n- ✅ **Transparent identification**: Clear User-Agent with contact info\\n- ✅ **No paywall circumvention**: Respect premium content\\n- ✅ **PII scrubbing**: Remove personal information\\n- ✅ **GDPR compliant**: Right to erasure, data retention limits\\n\\nSee [Ethical Crawling Guidelines](docs/architecture/ethical-crawling.md) for details.\\n\\n## Development\\n\\n### Project Structure\\n\\n```\\nShadowTag-v2-fastapi-services/\\n├── docs/\\n│   ├── architecture/          # Architecture documentation\\n│   │   ├── gemini-ingestion-layer.md\\n│   │   ├── ethical-crawling.md\\n│   │   └── tier-classification.md\\n│   └── prompts/               # Analysis prompts\\n│       └── gemini-ingestion-layer-analysis.md\\n├── src/\\n│   ├── api/                   # FastAPI endpoints\\n│   │   └── ingestion.py\\n│   ├── collectors/            # Source-specific collectors\\n│   ├── processors/            # Tier classification, quality scoring\\n│   └── utils/                 # Shared utilities\\n├── config/                    # Configuration files\\n│   ├── ethical-crawling.yaml\\n│   ├── tier-classification.yaml\\n│   ├── sources.yaml\\n│   └── quality-gates.yaml\\n├── k8s/                       # Kubernetes manifests\\n│   └── ingestion-cronjob.yaml\\n├── models/                    # ML models\\n├── data/                      # Data files\\n├── schemas/                   # JSON schemas\\n├── requirements.txt           # Python dependencies\\n├── package.json               # Node.js dependencies\\n└── README.md\\n```\\n\\n### Running Tests\\n\\n```bash\\npytest tests/ --cov=src --cov-report=html\\n```\\n\\n### Code Quality\\n\\n```bash\\n# Format code\\nblack src/\\nisort src/\\n\\n# Lint\\nflake8 src/\\nmypy src/\\n```\\n\\n## Deployment\\n\\n### GKE Deployment\\n\\n1. **Create namespace**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl create namespace pnkln-ingestion\\n   ```\\n\\n2. **Configure secrets**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl create secret generic ingestion-secrets \\\\\\n     --from-literal=youtube_api_key=YOUR_KEY \\\\\\n     --from-literal=twitter_bearer_token=YOUR_TOKEN \\\\\\n     --namespace pnkln-ingestion\\n   ```\\n\\n3. **Deploy CronJob**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl apply -f k8s/ingestion-cronjob.yaml\\n   ```\\n\\n4. **Monitor**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl get cronjobs -n pnkln-ingestion\\n   kubectl logs -f job/gemini-ingestion-layer-XXXXX -n pnkln-ingestion\\n   ```\\n\\n## Monitoring & Alerting\\n\\n### Prometheus Metrics\\n\\n- `ingestion_items_total{tier}`: Total items by tier\\n- `ingestion_runtime_seconds`: Job runtime\\n- `ingestion_cost_usd`: Operational cost\\n- `ingestion_relevance_score`: Average relevance\\n- `ingestion_errors_total{source}`: Errors by source\\n\\n### Grafana Dashboards\\n\\nImport dashboards from `dashboards/` directory:\\n- Tier Distribution Over Time\\n- Source Performance\\n- Cost Tracking\\n- Quality Metrics\\n\\n## Migration from Claude Code SDK\\n\\nThis project has been migrated from `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` to `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`. See [MIGRATION.md](MIGRATION.md) for details.\\n\\n## Documentation\\n\\n- [Architecture Overview](docs/architecture/gemini-ingestion-layer.md)\\n- [Ethical Crawling Guidelines](docs/architecture/ethical-crawling.md)\\n- [Tier Classification Model](docs/architecture/tier-classification.md)\\n- [Analysis Prompt](docs/prompts/gemini-ingestion-layer-analysis.md)\\n\\n## Contributing\\n\\n1. Fork the repository\\n2. Create a feature branch (`git checkout -b feature/amazing-feature`)\\n3. Commit your changes (`git commit -m 'Add amazing feature'`)\\n4. Push to the branch (`git push origin feature/amazing-feature`)\\n5. Open a Pull Request\\n\\n## License\\n\\nMIT License - see [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.\\n\\n## Support\\n\\n- **Issues**: https://github.com/ehanc69/ShadowTag-v2-fastapi-services/issues\\n- **Email**: contact@pnkln.ai\\n- **Docs**: https://pnkln.ai/docs\\n\\n## Acknowledgments\\n\\n- Part of the PNKLN Core Stack™\\n- Powered by Gemini 2.0 Pro\\n- Built with FastAPI, GKE, and Claude Agent SDK\\n\\n---\\n\\n**Status**: ✅ Production Ready (v1.0.0)\\n**Last Updated**: 2025-11-07\\n>>>>>>> claude/uninstall-claude-code-package-011CUuH5NYBC54NLvM9HYFcK\\n\",\"pastedContents\":{},\"timestamp\":1763500002105,\"project\":\"/Users/pikeymickey\",\"sessionId\":\"d46726b7-9ef2-47fb-9137-1cfb598a913a\"}\n{\"display\":\"# Node.js\\nnode_modules/\\n<<<<<<< claude/pnkln-intelligence-pipeline-01DwB3v8zwZaHZC3HogNeRXt\\n=======\\n\\n# Python\\n>>>>>>> claude/uninstall-claude-code-package-011CUuH5NYBC54NLvM9HYFcK\\n__pycache__/\\n*.py[cod]\\n*$py.class\\n*.so\\n.Python\\nbuild/\\ndevelop-eggs/\\ndist/\\ndownloads/\\neggs/\\n.eggs/\\nlib/\\nlib64/\\nparts/\\nsdist/\\nvar/\\nwheels/\\n<<<<<<< claude/pnkln-intelligence-pipeline-01DwB3v8zwZaHZC3HogNeRXt\\n*.egg-info/\\n.installed.cfg\\n*.egg\\n\\n# Virtual environments\\nvenv/\\nENV/\\nenv/\\n=======\\nshare/python-wheels/\\n*.egg-info/\\n.installed.cfg\\n*.egg\\nMANIFEST\\n\\n# Virtual environments\\nvenv/\\nenv/\\nENV/\\n>>>>>>> claude/uninstall-claude-code-package-011CUuH5NYBC54NLvM9HYFcK\\n.venv\\n\\n# IDE\\n.vscode/\\n.idea/\\n*.swp\\n*.swo\\n*~\\n<<<<<<< claude/pnkln-intelligence-pipeline-01DwB3v8zwZaHZC3HogNeRXt\\n\\n# Environment files\\n.env\\n.env.local\\n\\n# Logs\\nlogs/\\n*.log\\n\\n# Data and keys\\ndata/\\nkeys/\\n*.db\\n\\n# Test coverage\\n.coverage\\nhtmlcov/\\n.pytest_cache/\\n\\n# OS\\n.DS_Store\\nThumbs.di_tool/auth/credential_exchangers/test_service_account_exchanger.py:74:              private_key="-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----...",

   27→{\"display\":\"rror: GOOGLE_API_KEY not set\\nexport GOOGLE_API_KEY='your-key-here'\\n\\n# Verify it's set\\necho $GOOGLE_API_KEY\\n```\\n\\n### High Latency\\n\\nIf p99 latency >90ms:\\n1. Use `gemini-2.0-flash-exp` (fastest model)\\n2. Reduce function execution time\\n3. Check network latency\\n4. Consider local caching\\n\\n### Function Call Failures\\n\\nCommon issues:\\n- Parameter type mismatches → Check FunctionTool parameters\\n- Missing required params → Ensure all required params specified\\n- Function errors → Add try/catch to function implementations\\n\\n## 🤝 Contributing\\n\\nContributions welcome! Areas for improvement:\\n- Additional watermarking schemes\\n- Advanced semantic memory (vector DB integration)\\n- More JR validation strategies\\n- Performance optimizations\\n\\n## 📄 License\\n\\nMIT License - Free to use and modify\\n\\n## 🙏 Acknowledgments\\n\\n- Google Gemini Team for native function calling\\n- AutoGen for inspiration and baseline comparison\\n- PNKLN community for architecture guidance\\n\\n---\\n\\n**Built with ❤️ for the PNKLN Core Stack**\\n\\n**Questions?** Open an issue or check the examples!\\n=======\\n# Gemini Ingestion Layer - PNKLN Core Stack™\\n\\nIntelligent data collection and classification pipeline for the PNKLN Core Stack™. This service operates as a GKE CronJob that gathers intelligence from multiple sources nightly, classifies items by tier, and makes data available for downstream processing.\\n\\n## Overview\\n\\nThe Gemini Ingestion Layer is a foundational component that:\\n- Collects data from 6+ sources (YouTube, Twitter, News, RSS, Web, APIs)\\n- Classifies items into 3 tiers based on authority, relevance, and timeliness\\n- Ensures ethical crawling compliance (robots.txt, rate limiting)\\n- Delivers processed intelligence to Judge 6 and AM Briefing services\\n- Operates within a ~$77/month budget at ~45 minutes runtime per night\\n\\n## Architecture\\n\\n```\\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\\n│              Gemini Ingestion Layer (GKE)                   │\\n│                                                             │\\n│  ┌────────────┐  ┌────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐          │\\n│  │  YouTube   │  │  Twitter   │  │    News     │          │\\n│  │ Collector  │  │ Collector  │  │  Collector  │          │\\n│  └─────┬──────┘  └─────┬──────┘  └──────┬──────┘          │\\n│        │                │                 │                 │\\n│  ┌─────┴───────┬────────┴────────┬────────┴──────┐         │\\n│  │    RSS      │  Web Scraper    │ API Integrator│         │\\n│  │  Collector  │                 │               │         │\\n│  └─────┬───────┴────────┬────────┴────────┬──────┘         │\\n│        │                │                 │                 │\\n│        └────────────────┼─────────────────┘                 │\\n│                         │                                   │\\n│              ┌──────────▼──────────┐                        │\\n│              │ Tier Classification │                        │\\n│              │   (Tier 1/2/3)      │                        │\\n│              └──────────┬──────────┘                        │\\n│                         │                                   │\\n│              ┌──────────▼──────────┐                        │\\n│              │ Ethical Compliance  │                        │\\n│              │    Validation       │                        │\\n│              └──────────┬──────────┘                        │\\n│                         │                                   │\\n│              ┌──────────▼──────────┐                        │\\n│              │  Quality Scoring    │                        │\\n│              └──────────┬──────────┘                        │\\n│                         │                                   │\\n│              ┌──────────▼──────────┐                        │\\n│              │  Storage (GCS/DB)   │                        │\\n│              └─────────────────────┘                        │\\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\\n```\\n\\n## Features\\n\\n- **Multi-Source Collection**: YouTube, Twitter, News, RSS, Web scraping, API integrations\\n- **Intelligent Tier Classification**: ML-based classification (Tier 1/2/3)\\n- **Ethical Crawling**: 100% robots.txt compliance, adaptive rate limiting\\n- **Cost Efficient**: ~$77/month operational cost (~$0.012 per item)\\n- **FastAPI Integration**: REST API for triggering, querying, and monitoring\\n- **GKE Native**: Kubernetes CronJob with multi-container orchestration\\n- **Quality Gated**: Relevance, completeness, and timeliness checks\\n\\n## Quick Start\\n\\n### Prerequisites\\n\\n- Python 3.11+\\n- Google Cloud Platform account\\n- GKE cluster\\n- PostgreSQL database\\n- Redis instance\\n\\n### Installation\\n\\n1. **Clone the repository**:\\n   ```bash\\n   git clone https://github.com/ehanc69/ShadowTag-v2-fastapi-services.git\\n   cd ShadowTag-v2-fastapi-services\\n   ```\\n\\n2. **Install Python dependencies**:\\n   ```bash\\n   pip install -r requirements.txt\\n   ```\\n\\n3. **Configure environment**:\\n   ```bash\\n   cp .env.example .env\\n   # Edit .env with your credentials\\n   ```\\n\\n4. **Deploy to GKE**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl apply -f k8s/ingestion-cronjob.yaml\\n   ```\\n\\n### Running Locally\\n\\n**Start the FastAPI server**:\\n```bash\\nuvicorn src.api.ingestion:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\\n```\\n\\n**Access the API**:\\n- Swagger UI: http://localhost:8000/docs\\n- ReDoc: http://localhost:8000/redoc\\n\\n## API Endpoints\\n\\n### Core Endpoints\\n\\n| Method | Endpoint | Description |\\n|--------|----------|-------------|\\n| `POST` | `/ingestion/trigger` | Manually trigger ingestion job |\\n| `GET` | `/ingestion/status` | Get job status (current or by ID) |\\n| `GET` | `/ingestion/items` | Query ingested items (with filters) |\\n| `GET` | `/ingestion/metrics` | Performance metrics |\\n| `GET` | `/ingestion/sources` | Get source configuration |\\n| `POST` | `/ingestion/sources` | Update source configuration |\\n\\n### Example Usage\\n\\n**Trigger manual ingestion**:\\n```bash\\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/ingestion/trigger \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"reason\\\": \\\"Breaking news event\\\", \\\"override_schedule\\\": true}'\\n```\\n\\n**Query Tier 1 items**:\\n```bash\\ncurl \\\"http://localhost:8000/ingestion/items?tier=tier_1&limit=10\\\"\\n```\\n\\n**Get metrics**:\\n```bash\\ncurl \\\"http://localhost:8000/ingestion/metrics?days=7\\\"\\n```\\n\\n## Configuration\\n\\n### Source Configuration\\n\\nEdit `config/sources.yaml` to add/modify data sources:\\n\\n```yaml\\nsources:\\n  - type: youtube\\n    enabled: true\\n    channels:\\n      - UCxxxxxxxx\\n    max_videos_per_channel: 50\\n\\n  - type: twitter\\n    enabled: true\\n    keywords:\\n      - \\\"AI\\\"\\n      - \\\"machine learning\\\"\\n```\\n\\n### Tier Classification\\n\\nEdit `config/tier-classification.yaml` to adjust tier rules:\\n\\n```yaml\\ntier_1:\\n  min_authority: 90\\n  max_age_hours: 6\\n  min_relevance: 80\\n  min_engagement: 1000\\n```\\n\\n### Ethical Crawling\\n\\nEdit `config/ethical-crawling.yaml` to configure compliance:\\n\\n```yaml\\nrate_limiting:\\n  default_delay_seconds: 2\\n  adaptive: true\\n\\nrobots_txt:\\n  enabled: true\\n  respect_crawl_delay: true\\n```\\n\\n## Performance Metrics\\n\\n### Target SLAs\\n\\n| Metric | Target |\\n|--------|--------|\\n| Runtime | ≤45 minutes |\\n| Daily Items | 1000-5000 |\\n| Active Sources | ≥6 |\\n| Cost/Item | ≤$0.015 |\\n| Relevance (Avg) | ≥60/100 |\\n| Tier 1 % | 10-20% |\\n| Uptime | ≥99% |\\n\\n### Current Performance\\n\\nSee the [Gemini Ingestion Layer Analysis](docs/prompts/gemini-ingestion-layer-analysis.md) for detailed performance analysis.\\n\\n## Tier Definitions\\n\\n### Tier 1 (Priority Intelligence)\\n- High-authority sources (verified news, official channels)\\n- Time-critical (≤6 hours old)\\n- High relevance (≥80/100)\\n- Strong engagement (≥1000 signals)\\n- **Target**: 10-20% of total items\\n\\n### Tier 2 (Standard Intelligence)\\n- Moderate-authority sources\\n- Standard timeliness (≤72 hours)\\n- Moderate relevance (≥50/100)\\n- Moderate engagement (100-1000)\\n- **Target**: 30-40% of total items\\n\\n### Tier 3 (Background Intelligence)\\n- Lower-authority sources\\n- Older content (>72 hours)\\n- Tangential relevance (<50/100)\\n- Low engagement (<100)\\n- **Target**: 40-60% of total items\\n\\n## Ethical Guidelines\\n\\nWe adhere to strict ethical standards:\\n\\n- ✅ **100% robots.txt compliance**: Never violate crawler policies\\n- ✅ **Adaptive rate limiting**: Prevent server overload\\n- ✅ **Transparent identification**: Clear User-Agent with contact info\\n- ✅ **No paywall circumvention**: Respect premium content\\n- ✅ **PII scrubbing**: Remove personal information\\n- ✅ **GDPR compliant**: Right to erasure, data retention limits\\n\\nSee [Ethical Crawling Guidelines](docs/architecture/ethical-crawling.md) for details.\\n\\n## Development\\n\\n### Project Structure\\n\\n```\\nShadowTag-v2-fastapi-services/\\n├── docs/\\n│   ├── architecture/          # Architecture documentation\\n│   │   ├── gemini-ingestion-layer.md\\n│   │   ├── ethical-crawling.md\\n│   │   └── tier-classification.md\\n│   └── prompts/               # Analysis prompts\\n│       └── gemini-ingestion-layer-analysis.md\\n├── src/\\n│   ├── api/                   # FastAPI endpoints\\n│   │   └── ingestion.py\\n│   ├── collectors/            # Source-specific collectors\\n│   ├── processors/            # Tier classification, quality scoring\\n│   └── utils/                 # Shared utilities\\n├── config/                    # Configuration files\\n│   ├── ethical-crawling.yaml\\n│   ├── tier-classification.yaml\\n│   ├── sources.yaml\\n│   └── quality-gates.yaml\\n├── k8s/                       # Kubernetes manifests\\n│   └── ingestion-cronjob.yaml\\n├── models/                    # ML models\\n├── data/                      # Data files\\n├── schemas/                   # JSON schemas\\n├── requirements.txt           # Python dependencies\\n├── package.json               # Node.js dependencies\\n└── README.md\\n```\\n\\n### Running Tests\\n\\n```bash\\npytest tests/ --cov=src --cov-report=html\\n```\\n\\n### Code Quality\\n\\n```bash\\n# Format code\\nblack src/\\nisort src/\\n\\n# Lint\\nflake8 src/\\nmypy src/\\n```\\n\\n## Deployment\\n\\n### GKE Deployment\\n\\n1. **Create namespace**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl create namespace pnkln-ingestion\\n   ```\\n\\n2. **Configure secrets**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl create secret generic ingestion-secrets \\\\\\n     --from-literal=youtube_api_key=YOUR_KEY \\\\\\n     --from-literal=twitter_bearer_token=YOUR_TOKEN \\\\\\n     --namespace pnkln-ingestion\\n   ```\\n\\n3. **Deploy CronJob**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl apply -f k8s/ingestion-cronjob.yaml\\n   ```\\n\\n4. **Monitor**:\\n   ```bash\\n   kubectl get cronjobs -n pnkln-ingestion\\n   kubectl logs -f job/gemini-ingestion-layer-XXXXX -n pnkln-ingestion\\n   ```\\n\\n## Monitoring & Alerting\\n\\n### Prometheus Metrics\\n\\n- `ingestion_items_total{tier}`: Total items by tier\\n- `ingestion_runtime_seconds`: Job runtime\\n- `ingestion_cost_usd`: Operational cost\\n- `ingestion_relevance_score`: Average relevance\\n- `ingestion_errors_total{source}`: Errors by source\\n\\n### Grafana Dashboards\\n\\nImport dashboards from `dashboards/` directory:\\n- Tier Distribution Over Time\\n- Source Performance\\n- Cost Tracking\\n- Quality Metrics\\n\\n## Migration from Claude Code SDK\\n\\nThis project has been migrated from `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` to `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`. See [MIGRATION.md](MIGRATION.md) for details.\\n\\n## Documentation\\n\\n- [Architecture Overview](docs/architecture/gemini-ingestion-layer.md)\\n- [Ethical Crawling Guidelines](docs/architecture/ethical-crawling.md)\\n- [Tier Classification Model](docs/architecture/tier-classification.md)\\n- [Analysis Prompt](docs/prompts/gemini-ingestion-layer-analysis.md)\\n\\n## Contributing\\n\\n1. Fork the repository\\n2. Create a feature branch (`git checkout -b feature/amazing-feature`)\\n3. Commit your changes (`git commit -m 'Add amazing feature'`)\\n4. Push to the branch (`git push origin feature/amazing-feature`)\\n5. Open a Pull Request\\n\\n## License\\n\\nMIT License - see [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.\\n\\n## Support\\n\\n- **Issues**: https://github.com/ehanc69/ShadowTag-v2-fastapi-services/issues\\n- **Email**: contact@pnkln.ai\\n- **Docs**: https://pnkln.ai/docs\\n\\n## Acknowledgments\\n\\n- Part of the PNKLN Core Stack™\\n- Powered by Gemini 2.0 Pro\\n- Built with FastAPI, GKE, and Claude Agent SDK\\n\\n---\\n\\n**Status**: ✅ Production Ready (v1.0.0)\\n**Last Updated**: 2025-11-07\\n>>>>>>>
